api.name: OpenAI Responses API
api.instructions_field: carries the base system prompt (recorded in session_meta.payload.base_instructions.text)

input_item_types.use: AGENTS.md injection, <environment_context>, then real user turn

api.rollout_log_note: ~/.codex/sessions/YYYY/MM/DD/rollout-*.jsonl lines are {timestamp,type:'response_item',payload:<ITEM>}; payload IS the wire item. event_msg/turn_context/session_meta lines are local telemetry.

system_prompt.verbatim_or_path: codex-rs/core/gpt_5_2_prompt.md | codex-rs/core/gpt-5.2-codex_prompt.md | codex-rs/core/prompt_with_apply_patch_instructions.md | (also gpt_5_1_prompt.md, gpt-5.1-codex-max_prompt.md, gpt_5_codex_prompt.md). Selected by model family. Full verbatim text fetched into codex.md.

opening_lines.gpt_5_2_prompt.md: You are GPT-5.2 running in the Codex CLI, a terminal-based coding assistant. Codex CLI is an open source project led by OpenAI. You are expected to be precise, safe, and helpful.
opening_lines.gpt-5.2-codex_prompt.md: You are Codex, based on GPT-5. You are running as a coding agent in the Codex CLI on a user's computer.
opening_lines.prompt_with_apply_patch_instructions.md: You are a coding agent running in the Codex CLI, a terminal-based coding assistant. Codex CLI is an open source project led by OpenAI. You are expected to be precise, safe, and helpful.

major_sections: Presenting your work and final message
Final answer structure and style guidelines
system_prompt.override_note: When config sets model_instructions_file, base_instructions.text is the user's file, not the stock prompt.

tool_definition.namespacing: Responses tool names must match [A-Za-z0-9_]; every other char -> '_' (sanitize_responses_api_tool_name). MCP delimiter '__'. Modern form: bare de-dashed name + sibling 'namespace':'mcp__<server>' and tool nested under {'type':'namespace','name':'mcp__<server>','tools':[...]}. Legacy flat form: 'mcp__<server>__<tool>', 64-byte cap, SHA-1(12-hex) collision suffix. e.g. argent list-devices -> name 'list_devices', namespace 'mcp__argent'.
tool_definition.schema_format: Flattened Responses-API: {'type':'function','name','description','strict','parameters':<JsonSchema>}. NOT Chat-Completions {'type':'function','function':{...}}. strict=false for MCP/dynamic tools. defer_loading omitted when None. output_schema is serde(skip) -> never on wire. apply_patch is a freeform {'type':'custom'} Lark-grammar tool.

example.name: exec_command
example.description: Runs a command in a PTY, returning output or a session ID for ongoing interaction.

workdir.description: Working directory for the command. Defaults to the turn cwd.

yield_time_ms.description: Wait before yielding output. Defaults to 10000 ms; effective range is 250-30000 ms.

max_output_tokens.description: Output token budget. Defaults to 10000 tokens; larger requests may be capped by policy.

apply_patch_example.name: apply_patch
apply_patch_example.description: Use the `apply_patch` tool to edit files. This is a FREEFORM tool, so do not wrap the patch in JSON.

format.definition:
start: begin_patch hunk+ end_patch
begin_patch: "*** Begin Patch" LF
... (apply_patch.lark)

assistant_tool_call_format.shape: {'type':'function_call','name':<string>,'arguments':<JSON-encoded STRING>,'call_id':'call_<22alnum>'} plus optional 'namespace':'mcp__<server>' for MCP tools. arguments is always a string even when empty ('{}').

tool_result_format.shape: {'type':'function_call_output','call_id':<same call_id>,'output':<STRING or LIST>}. output is a string for text results (shell + text-only MCP), or a LIST of {input_text|input_image} parts for multimodal (screenshots).
tool_result_format.mcp_wrapper_note: MCP outputs omit 'Command:', 'Process exited with code', and 'Original token count'; body is the JSON-serialized MCP content array, not bare text.
tool_result_format.image_handling: Multimodal MCP outputs return output as a LIST. Image part = {'type':'input_image','image_url':'data:image/png;base64,<...>','detail':'high'}. Preceded by literal text part '--- Screen after action ---' and followed by a 'Saved: <abs path>.png' text part. Text parts use type 'input_text'.
tool_result_format.truncation: Output truncation elides the MIDDLE (head+tail kept) and inserts the literal Unicode-ellipsis marker '…<N> tokens truncated…' (regex: …\d+ tokens truncated…). Budget = max_output_tokens (default 10000). NOT a head/tail '[... omitted N lines ...]' style marker.

example.output:
Command: /bin/zsh -lc 'git status --short --branch'
Chunk ID: d620f5
Wall time: 0.0000 seconds
Process exited with code 0
Original token count: 17
Output:
## HEAD (no branch)
/Users/x/argent

other_junk:
<permissions instructions>
Filesystem sandboxing defines which files can be read or written. `sandbox_mode` is `danger-full-access`: No filesystem sandboxing - all commands are permitted. Network access is enabled.
Approval policy is currently never. Do not provide the `sandbox_permissions` for any reason, commands will be rejected.
</permissions instructions>
newer main environment_context.rs renders richer XML: <filesystem>/<workspace_roots>/<permission_profile>/<network enabled="true">/<approval_policy>, with &/</>/"/' XML-escaping
capture_method: 1) BEST: read ~/.codex/sessions/YYYY/MM/DD/rollout-*.jsonl — each {type:'response_item',payload} line is a verbatim Responses input[] item (jq -c 'select(.type=="response_item")|.payload'). 2) Proxy POST /v1/responses via HTTPS_PROXY/mitmproxy or OPENAI_BASE_URL to capture the raw request body (instructions+input+tools). 3) `codex exec "<prompt>"` for a clean one-shot, then read its fresh rollout file or the proxy. 4) Repo cross-check: gh api repos/openai/codex/contents/<path>?ref=main -H 'Accept: application/vnd.github.raw'.
notes: Rollout .jsonl payloads ARE the wire items — no proxy needed.
Two output wrappers: heavy shell (Command/Chunk ID/Wall time/Process exited with code/Original token count/Output) vs light MCP (Wall time/Output + raw MCP content-array JSON).
MCP tool calls in 0.135 carry a 'namespace' field (mcp__argent) plus a de-dashed bare name; dashes illegal in Responses tool names.
Truncation elides the MIDDLE with '…N tokens truncated…' (Unicode ellipsis), not a head/tail '[... omitted ...]'.
Screenshots return as a multimodal output LIST with literal '--- Screen after action ---' separator + trailing 'Saved: <path>' text part; image detail 'high'.
apply_patch is a 'type':'custom' Lark-grammar freeform tool, not a JSON function.
reasoning items round-trip via encrypted_content (gAAAA... blob).
Tools are flattened Responses-API ({type:function,name,...}), not Chat-Completions nested.
